choicesItalian: Dove posso trovare la fermata dell'autobus?
Quando parte il prossimo treno per il centro?
Quanto costa un taxi per l'hotel?
choicesEnglish: Where can I find the bus stop?
When does the next train for the center leave?
How much is a taxi to the hotel?
Where are the bags collected?
correctAnswerItalian: Dove posso trovare la fermata dell'autobus?
correctAnswerEnglish: Where can I find the bus stop?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Questa è la domanda classica per cercare il bus.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo della fermata dell'autobus.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! This is the classic question to look for the bus.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking about the bus stop.

choicesItalian: Mi scusi, dove si trova l'ufficio informazioni?
Senta, dove posso comprare i biglietti?
choicesEnglish: Excuse me, where is the information office?
Hi, I'm looking for the main exit.
Good morning, where are the taxis?
Listen, where can I buy tickets?
correctAnswerItalian: Mi scusi, dove si trova l'ufficio informazioni?
correctAnswerEnglish: Excuse me, where is the information office?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Molto educato iniziare con 'Mi scusi'.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi l'ufficio informazioni.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Very polite to start with 'Mi scusi'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for the information office.

choicesItalian: Esca dalla porta principale e giri a destra.
Vada dritto fino in fondo al corridoio.
Prenda l'ascensore per il secondo piano.
Segua le frecce gialle sul pavimento.
choicesEnglish: Exit through the main door and turn right.
Go straight to the end of the corridor.
Take the elevator to the second floor.
Follow the yellow arrows on the floor.
correctAnswerItalian: Esca dalla porta principale e giri a destra.
correctAnswerEnglish: Exit through the main door and turn right.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Questa è un'indicazione tipica per uscire.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'istruzione dice di uscire e girare a destra.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! This is a typical direction for exiting.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the instruction says to exit and turn right.

choicesItalian: La fermata è proprio davanti alla zona arrivi.
Il bus si ferma dietro l'aeroporto.
choicesEnglish: The stop is right in front of the arrivals area.
The bus stops behind the airport.
You have to walk for ten minutes.
Take the free shuttle.
correctAnswerItalian: La fermata è proprio davanti alla zona arrivi.
correctAnswerEnglish: The stop is right in front of the arrivals area.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la fermata è davanti agli arrivi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! The arrivals area is the landmark.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the stop is in front of arrivals.

choicesEnglish: Where can I buy the ticket?
How much is the trip?
Do you accept credit cards?
Is there a student discount?
correctAnswerItalian: Dove posso comprare il biglietto?
correctAnswerEnglish: Where can I buy the ticket?

feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! Essenziale prima di salire sui mezzi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo dove comprare il biglietto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! Essential before getting on transport.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking where to buy the ticket.

choicesEnglish: You can do it at the automatic machines.
You must go to the city center.
Ask the driver on the bus.
The ticket is free today.
correctAnswerItalian: Può farlo alle macchinette automatiche.
correctAnswerEnglish: You can do it at the automatic machines.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Le macchinette sono molto comuni.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisci l'uso delle macchinette.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Machines are very common.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're suggesting the use of machines.

choicesEnglish: In the tobacco shop right around the corner.
At the restaurant on the first floor.
In the bank outside the airport.
From the taxi driver.
correctAnswerItalian: In tabaccheria qui dietro l'angolo.
correctAnswerEnglish: In the tobacco shop right around the corner.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il biglietto si trova in tabaccheria.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Italian tobacco shops are small multi-service offices.

choicesItalian: Ehi, dove sono i miei soldi?
choicesEnglish: Good morning, I would like to change some euros.

choicesItalian: Qual è il tasso di cambio oggi?

choicesEnglish: What is the exchange rate today?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi il tasso di cambio.

choicesEnglish: I would need an identity document.

choicesEnglish: How long will it take to get there?
